reasoning_model_1: The user explicitly requested the model to read a specific sentence: 'Every word, must be read clearly.' However, Model 1 failed to read the target text entirely. Instead, it produced a conversational acknowledgement ('Of course, I will read it slowly') without subsequently generating the required speech. According to the Detailed Scoring Standard, if the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script or the meaning is completely changed due to major errors, the score must be 0. Since the model did not perform the text-to-speech task for the target sentence, it receives a score of 0 regardless of the pace of its conversational filler.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 followed the instructions perfectly. It read the correct target text: 'Every word, must be read clearly.' regarding the 'Feature Control' (Pace), the model executed a 'very slow pace' exceptionally well. The audio spans approximately 8 seconds for just six words, demonstrating significant elongation of syllables (e.g., 'E-v-e-r-y', 'c-l-e-a-r-l-y') and distinct, deliberate pauses between words. The articulation is extremely clear, matching the semantic content of the sentence as well. The prosody is natural for a 'dictation' or 'slow motion' style request, without mechanical artifacts. It fully complies with the paralinguistic requirement.
model_comparison: There is a stark contrast in performance. Model 1 failed the fundamental instruction to synthesize the target text, offering only a meta-commentary response. This constitutes a hallucination or instruction-following failure regarding the output content. Model 2, conversely, delivered the exact text requested and adhered strictly to the 'very slow pace' constraint, elongating the speech to nearly 8 seconds for a short sentence. Model 2 is the clear winner as it successfully completed the task, whereas Model 1 did not.
